blocks.content: Most of the Black Line are wanted criminals.
The Seven Sins in particular were branded as world-class atrocious criminals, so they absolutely need a safe hideout to stay in one place.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Working under such a lacking master, it's only a matter of time before one finds their neck on the guillotine.
As this thought crossed his mind, he couldn't help but worry about his own safety.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Hmm, maybe it's time to wrap things up here?

The reason bounty hunters from the Red Line, who had been tracking him with crazed eyes, had never caught Arius until now was thanks to his beast-like instinct for knowing exactly when to strike and when to flee.
His intuition, honed by over a decade as a fugitive, was warning him that if he didn't leave now, it would be dangerous.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: But it's a shame to end it like this....
Ataraxia. The biggest catch in his grave robber career had been ensnared in his net.
Though material wealth played a part in why he dove into grave robbing, the greater motivator was to satisfy the intellectual hunger of an Unlocker.
In that sense, the power of Archangel Ikael was certainly something that piqued his curiosity.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Should I just act crazy and dive down to the first level? No, it's impossible alone. Especially since a mage's unconscious is much stronger than ordinary people's. I can't rely on Orkamph either.
As Arius propped his chin and sank into thought, he slowly raised his head.
Glancing down the empty hallway, he flashed a thin smile and spoke.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: What's the matter?
When an unknown official close to Zion revealed himself from the corner, a sneer naturally formed.
The official was neither a mage nor an assassin. But as always, his presence couldn't be felt until he showed signs of himself first.

blocks.speaker: Official
blocks.content: There's someone who wishes to meet you, Sir Arius.
Arius knew who Zion was. But the official refrained from mentioning his master's name.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: That's a welcome proposition. Lead the way.
As the administrator turned around and took a step, Arius stared at the old man's Achilles tendon.
He walked briskly, not making a sound.
Despite appearing as light as a cat, the calves hidden beneath the robe were undoubtedly highly developed.
However, that didn't necessarily mean he was a martial arts master. It was more like the craftsmanship of someone who had spent a lifetime tending to their master's needs.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Haha, what an interesting place. The Royal Palace.
The glass bottle Zion threw smashed against the gallery door.
BOOM
The shattered glass flew everywhere, startling the cat sleeping on the floor into the air.
Unbothered, Uorin, sitting at the table, raised a teacup to her lips and took a sip.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Why? I thought it was great. It was quite spectacular.
Zion glared at his sister.
Her nonchalant personality was usually cute, but in a situation like this, it's so infuriating he could die.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: I'll have to step up myself. I can't leave Shirone alone.
Uorin stroked the chin of the cat that climbed onto her lap and said.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: He was threatening though. What if he had cast magic towards the audiences' seats?
Zion flushed as he recalled the situation at the time.
As Uorin said, it was a terrifying force. No matter how top-notch the guards were, there would have been casualties.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: That's exactly it! It was a declaration of war! It must have been revenge for me pointing a sword at him!

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Isn't that a bit of an overstatement? He could have just done that because he was angry.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: It's the same either way! That lowlife ignored my offer! I won't let it slide!
knock
Just then, someone knocked on the door.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Come in.
Arius opened the door and entered.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: It's been a while, First Prince. Ah, you were here too, Young Lady.
Zion snorted.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Sit down for now. There's something I want to discuss.
As Arius gestured for understanding and sat down on the chair, Uorin poured him some tea.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: What brings you to summon me? Have you grown tired of the Objet I procured last time?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Ei, you didn't 'procure' it. You sold it to us, fair and square.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Haha! If we're being precise, that's right. But you're well aware that it's not something that can simply be bought with money, yes? The Objet.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: The Objet isn't the issue.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Oh? Then what is it?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Don't play dumb! It's clear now. The reason father summoned Shirone to the palace. It's to rob Acraxia, right?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: It's not Acraxia, it's Ataraxia. And grave robbing, really. If you're going to say it, please call it extraction.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: So... how far has the plan progressed?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Hmm, even for the First Prince, this is a bit difficult. After all, I am a subject of His Majesty, so I can't just reveal classified information.
Zion extended his hand to the north.
Armand flew into his hand as if sucked in by a magnetic force, and in one swift motion, he pointed it directly at Arius's throat.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: I'm not joking around right now. It's best you answer properly.
Arius took a leisurely sip of tea, as if only now feeling at ease with the sword aimed at him.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Zion was nothing more than a brat without manners. Armand, on the other hand, was a troublesome weapon.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: If you're asking how far it has progressed... about 49 percent, I'd say?
It wasn't a lie. After all, starting is half the work, and since they haven't even started yet, 49 percent is appropriate.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Uorin let out a small chuckle as she realized the meaning of the words, but Zion seemed to think that Arius had made considerable progress and continued to press him with a surprised expression.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Stop it at all costs. No, just eliminate Ataraxia completely. If you're a grave robber, it should be possible, right?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: What a pathetic bastard. How could he think of eliminating the power of an archangel that has crossed into the world just because a mere king deems it necessary?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Ataraxia, even if he didn't excavate it, was a treasure trove for humanity that had to be analyzed at some point.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: I'll give you as much money as you want.
Arius closed his mouth.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: He had earned a huge fortune while dealing with Zion so far. Of course, he had a tendency to enjoy grave robbing itself rather than money, but he intends to part ways with Orkamph anyway. If he made a big haul one last time and left, wouldn't he be able to secure something like a retirement fund?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Hmm.
Lost in thought, Arius finally revealed his true nature.
As his cold, snake-like eyes emerged, Zion, despite having a sword pointed at him, couldn't help but shiver.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: However, the pupils hidden by Arius' narrow eyes were not directed at Zion, but at Uorin.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Beautiful.
It wasn't an emotion one would normally feel for a fourteen-year-old girl, but she was truly stunning.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Her mysterious appearance, combined with the halo of being a Terraje, made her a truly tempting fruit.
He had analyzed the minds of countless individuals, but Uorin remained the only one whose thoughts he couldn't fathom at all.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: If he could dive one last time before he died, the target would be Uorin, not Ataraxia.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: First, let me tell you the exact progress. Currently, it's impossible to extract Shirone's Ataraxia. It is encapsulated. However, if you wish, I can extract Ataraxia in its entirety and present it to you, Prince.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Present it to me? Ataraxia?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Yes. The power of an archangel would belong to Your Highness.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: If I bring you Shirone's head, can you extract Ataraxia?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: If you're asking whether it's possible, yes, it is. But it must be done quickly. The moment the head is severed, I need to Dive immediately. Because 'if by any chance' it leads to death, the mind also disappears.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: That fast? Life would only last about 10 seconds after decapitation.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Time flows slowly in the unconscious. The door has been installed, so as soon as Shirone is decapitated, I will Dive. I'll Dive to the first level, and from there, I'll excavate the true Ataraxia. Then it will belong to Your Highness.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: No matter what, an outright assassination. Isn't that too hasty?

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Arius read the murderous intent that appeared in Zion's eyes and smiled with satisfaction.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: So, how much are you willing to pay?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: 2.7 billion gold.

blocks.speaker: Arius
blocks.content: Hmm, wasn't Armand 3 billion gold?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: So what? Are you saying that bastard's magic is more valuable than my magic sword?
